1	/**
2	 * Canonical vocabulary for `Upload.status`.
3	 *
4	 * The four original values — `pending | processing | ingested | failed`
5	 * — are load-bearing strings from the init migration. Everything that
6	 * ever wrote to `Upload.status` (the POST /api/upload handler, the
7	 * trigger-processing dispatcher, every branch of
8	 * noosphere/codex_bridge.py, the library filter dropdown) agrees on
9	 * them. DO NOT rename.
10	 *
11	 * The two new values (`extracting`, `awaiting_ingest`) were added in
12	 * Wave 1 so founders can see which stage of the pipeline their upload
13	 * is in:
14	 *
15	 *   pending          → queued, the local noosphere runner hasn't
16	 *                      picked it up yet
17	 *   extracting       → transcribing audio / extracting PDF text; no
18	 *                      LLM calls in this stage
19	 *   awaiting_ingest  → text is ready, about to hand off to claim
20	 *                      extraction
21	 *   processing       → claim extraction in progress (may call the LLM)
22	 *   ingested         → conclusions written to the Codex
23	 *   failed           → see `errorMessage`; retryable from the dashboard
24	 *
25	 * The legacy `queued_offline` value (older self-hosted flow where no
26	 * GitHub Actions dispatch was configured) is intentionally NOT in the
27	 * list — it's equivalent to `pending` for display purposes and gets
28	 * treated as such via {@link normalizeStatus}.
29	 */
30	
31	export const UPLOAD_STATUSES = [
32	  "pending",
33	  "extracting",
34	  "awaiting_ingest",
35	  "processing",
36	  "ingested",
37	  "failed",
38	] as const;
39	export type UploadStatus = (typeof UPLOAD_STATUSES)[number];
40	
41	export const STATUS_LABEL: Record<UploadStatus, string> = {
42	  pending: "queued",
43	  extracting: "extracting text",
44	  awaiting_ingest: "awaiting ingest",
45	  processing: "extracting claims",
46	  ingested: "ingested",
47	  failed: "failed",
48	};
49	
50	export const STATUS_COLOR: Record<UploadStatus, string> = {
51	  // Muted parchment — the row has arrived but the pipeline hasn't
52	  // touched it yet. Using a CSS var so the pill adapts to theme swaps.
53	  pending: "var(--parchment-dim, #8a8675)",
54	  // Amber family — active, non-terminal, all three stages share the
55	  // colour so the founder reads "the pipeline is working" without
56	  // tracking which specific stage we're in.
57	  extracting: "var(--amber, #c9944a)",
58	  awaiting_ingest: "var(--amber, #c9944a)",
59	  processing: "var(--amber, #c9944a)",
60	  // Moss green — terminal success.
61	  ingested: "#497a3d",
62	  // Ember red — terminal failure; matches the existing --ember token.
63	  failed: "var(--ember, #b33a2a)",
64	};
65	
66	export const STATUS_TOOLTIP: Record<UploadStatus, string> = {
67	  pending: "Waiting for the local noosphere runner to pick this up.",
68	  extracting: "Transcribing audio or extracting PDF text. No LLM calls yet.",
69	  awaiting_ingest: "Text is ready; waiting for claim extraction to start.",
70	  processing: "Claim extraction in progress (may call the LLM).",
71	  ingested: "Conclusions have been written to your Codex.",
72	  failed: "Processing failed. See the error detail and retry.",
73	};
74	
75	/**
76	 * Non-terminal statuses animate in the UI so the founder has a visible
77	 * "pipeline is alive" cue. Excludes `pending` because a row can sit in
78	 * `pending` for a while (GitHub Actions queue depth, local runner
79	 * cadence) and a pulse there would imply more activity than is
80	 * actually happening.
81	 */
82	export const PULSING_STATUSES: ReadonlySet<UploadStatus> = new Set<UploadStatus>([
83	  "extracting",
84	  "awaiting_ingest",
85	  "processing",
86	]);
87	
88	export function isUploadStatus(s: string): s is UploadStatus {
89	  return (UPLOAD_STATUSES as readonly string[]).includes(s);
90	}
91	
92	/**
93	 * Map legacy / unknown status strings to a known UploadStatus. Anything
94	 * not in the canonical list falls back to `pending` so the badge still
95	 * renders (the founder sees "queued" rather than a raw / blank pill).
96	 */
97	export function normalizeStatus(raw: string | null | undefined): UploadStatus {
98	  if (!raw) return "pending";
99	  if (isUploadStatus(raw)) return raw;
100	  if (raw === "queued_offline") return "pending";
101	  return "pending";
102	}
103

1	generator client {
2	  provider = "prisma-client-js"
3	  // No custom `output` — Prisma 7 puts types alongside the runtime at
4	  // `node_modules/.prisma/client` automatically when you leave this blank,
5	  // and `@prisma/client` re-exports them. Explicitly setting
6	  // `output = "../node_modules/.prisma/client"` used to work on Prisma 6
7	  // but in Prisma 7 it only emits the WASM runtime (no .d.ts files),
8	  // which breaks every `import { PrismaClient } from "@prisma/client"`.
9	}
10	
11	// Postgres everywhere (Supabase / Neon / local Docker Postgres). Prisma bakes
12	// the provider into the generated migrations, so you can't switch to SQLite
13	// per-env with the same migration history. Use a local Postgres container
14	// (see `docker-compose.dev.yml`) for offline dev, or point DATABASE_URL at
15	// your Supabase project for both dev and prod.
16	datasource db {
17	  provider = "postgresql"
18	}
19	
20	// ── Multi-tenant root (cloud: Postgres + RLS; local dev: SQLite) ─────────────
21	
22	model Organization {
23	  id        String    @id @default(cuid())
24	  /// URL-safe tenant key (login, subdomains, worker routing)
25	  slug      String    @unique
26	  name      String
27	  /// Soft-delete grace window (hard-delete after policy window)
28	  deletedAt DateTime?
29	  createdAt DateTime  @default(now())
30	  updatedAt DateTime  @updatedAt
31	
32	  founders             Founder[]
33	  sessions             Session[]
34	  uploads              Upload[]
35	  conclusions          Conclusion[]
36	  contradictions       Contradiction[]
37	  driftEvents          DriftEvent[]
38	  researchSuggestions  ResearchSuggestion[]
39	  reviewItems          ReviewItem[]
40	  openQuestions        OpenQuestion[]
41	  auditEvents          AuditEvent[]
42	  publicationReviews   PublicationReview[]
43	  publishedConclusions PublishedConclusion[]
44	  publicResponses      PublicResponse[]
45	  apiKeys              ApiKey[]
46	}
47	
48	// ── Founders (portal users) — maps to FounderProfile in noosphere/models.py ─
49	
50	model Founder {
51	  id             String       @id @default(cuid())
52	  organizationId String
53	  organization   Organization @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
54	
55	  name         String
56	  username     String
57	  email        String
58	  passwordHash String
59	  // Role ladder — see src/lib/roles.ts for the predicate helpers and
60	  // the full description of each tier:
61	  //   admin   → full read + write + can change other founders' roles
62	  //   founder → full read + write
63	  //   viewer  → read-only; every write API returns 403 and every UI
64	  //              affordance for writes is hidden
65	  // Default "founder" preserves prior behaviour: anyone added via
66	  // scripts/add-founder.ts is read+write unless explicitly seeded
67	  // with ADD_FOUNDER_ROLE=viewer or =admin. Validation lives at the
68	  // application layer; the column stays TEXT so adding a future tier
69	  // is a docstring + helper change, not a migration.
70	  role         String  @default("founder") // admin | founder | viewer
71	  bio          String?
72	  avatar       String?
73	
74	  /// Maps to FounderProfile.id in Noosphere
75	  noosphereId String?
76	
77	  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
78	  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt
79	
80	  sessions                   Session[]
81	  uploads                    Upload[]
82	  auditEvents                AuditEvent[]
83	  deletionRequests           DeletionRequest[]
84	  attributedConclusions      Conclusion[]                @relation("ConclusionFounder")
85	  researchSuggestions        ResearchSuggestion[]
86	  reviewResolutions          ReviewItem[]                @relation("ReviewResolver")
87	  publicationReviews         PublicationReview[]         @relation("PublicationReviewer")
88	  apiKeys                    ApiKey[]
89	  conclusionDeletionRequests ConclusionDeletionRequest[]
90	  dashboardDismissals        DashboardDismissal[]
91	  resolvedContradictions     Contradiction[]             @relation("ContradictionResolver")
92	
93	  @@unique([organizationId, email])
94	  @@unique([organizationId, username])
95	  @@index([organizationId])
96	}
97	
98	/// Browser session (server-side row; cookie carries signed opaque token)
99	model Session {
100	  id             String   @id @default(cuid())
101	  organizationId String
102	  founderId      String
103	  token          String   @unique
104	  expiresAt      DateTime
105	  createdAt      DateTime @default(now())
106	
107	  founder      Founder      @relation(fields: [founderId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
108	  organization Organization @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
109	
110	  @@index([organizationId])
111	  @@index([founderId])
112	}
113	
114	// ── Uploads ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
115	
116	model Upload {
117	  id             String @id @default(cuid())
118	  organizationId String
119	  founderId      String
120	
121	  title            String
122	  description      String?
123	  sourceType       String   @default("written") // written | annotation | external | transcript
124	  originalName     String
125	  mimeType         String
126	  filePath         String
127	  fileSize         Int
128	  textContent      String?
129	  // Status ladder (see src/lib/uploadStatus.ts for the canonical
130	  // vocabulary + UI labels). `pending | processing | ingested | failed`
131	  // are load-bearing strings from the init migration; `extracting` and
132	  // `awaiting_ingest` were added alongside the Wave-1 audio/PDF
133	  // extractors so founders can see which stage of the pipeline a row is
134	  // in (Whisper/pypdf vs. claim extraction) instead of a single opaque
135	  // "processing".
136	  status           String   @default("pending") // pending | extracting | awaiting_ingest | processing | ingested | failed
137	  processLog       String   @default("") // streamed CLI stdout/stderr
138	  claimsCount      Int?
139	  methodCount      Int?
140	  substCount       Int?
141	  principleCount   Int?
142	  errorMessage     String?
143	  // How the text was obtained for this upload, set by the noosphere
144	  // extractor dispatcher on success. Free-form string — e.g.
145	  // "passthrough", "faster-whisper", "openai-whisper-1", "pypdf",
146	  // "ocrmypdf". Rendered in the dashboard detail row so the founder
147	  // knows whether a transcript/PDF extract was reliable.
148	  extractionMethod String?
149	  createdAt        DateTime @default(now())
150	  updatedAt        DateTime @updatedAt
151	
152	  // ── Public blog fields ─────────────────────────────────────────────
153	  // An Upload becomes a public blog post when `publishedAt IS NOT NULL`.
154	  // Default is null → the upload remains a private founder artifact.
155	  // `slug` is the URL-safe identifier used for `/post/:slug`; it's
156	  // generated from `title` at publish time and guaranteed unique across
157	  // the whole table (not per-org — the blog is a single public surface).
158	  // `blogExcerpt` is the card/list summary shown on the index; if null
159	  // we derive one from the first ~280 chars of `textContent`.
160	  // `authorBio` lets the founder override the displayed byline (e.g.
161	  // for guest posts or co-authored work); otherwise we show
162	  // Founder.name.
163	  publishedAt DateTime?
164	  slug        String?   @unique
165	  blogExcerpt String?
166	  authorBio   String?
167	
168	  // ── Soft delete ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
169	  // `deletedAt` is the single source of truth for "this upload has
170	  // been removed". Every query that surfaces uploads to the UI must
171	  // filter by `deletedAt IS NULL`; we keep the row around so the audit
172	  // trail (AuditEvent pointing at this upload) stays intact, and so a
173	  // DB-level restore is possible if a deletion turns out to be a
174	  // mistake. Only the uploader can set this directly; peers must go
175	  // through a DeletionRequest, which the owner accepts (setting
176	  // `deletedAt=now`) or declines.
177	  deletedAt DateTime?
178	
179	  // ── Visibility inside the firm ─────────────────────────────────────
180	  // Three-level visibility ladder. Every value still lets Noosphere
181	  // run ingestion — conclusions, contradictions, open questions, and
182	  // research suggestions are always extracted. The levels only
183	  // control *who reads the upload's metadata and contents*.
184	  //
185	  // "org"          → visible to every founder in the organization AND
186	  //                   eligible for blog publication. The default;
187	  //                   library, dashboard, peer deletion requests, and
188	  //                   the blog's publishAsPost toggle all apply.
189	  //
190	  // "semi-private" → visible to every founder in the organization,
191	  //                   but never publishable to the public blog. The
192	  //                   intermediate level, for material the firm wants
193	  //                   to discuss internally but that must never leak
194	  //                   outside. /api/publish rejects the flip; the
195	  //                   public blog index + /post/[slug] both filter
196	  //                   it out; /library and /dashboard render it with
197	  //                   a "semi-private" badge so peers know the
198	  //                   constraint.
199	  //
200	  // "private"      → only the uploader can see the row anywhere in
201	  //                   the UI; Noosphere still ingests it so the owner
202	  //                   gets their own conclusions/contradictions, but
203	  //                   nobody else can see the file, read it, or open
204	  //                   a deletion request against it. Blog-publishing
205	  //                   a private upload is rejected at the API layer
206	  //                   — flip visibility first.
207	  //
208	  // Column stays TEXT with default "org" so no migration is needed
209	  // when new levels are introduced; validation happens at the API
210	  // layer.
211	  visibility String @default("org")
212	
213	  // ── Podcast / audio playback ───────────────────────────────────────
214	  // When an audio upload is stored in a public CDN-addressable
215	  // location (Supabase Storage in our case), we persist the public
216	  // URL here so /post/[slug] can render a <audio controls> player
217	  // alongside the transcript. `audioDurationSec` is optional
218	  // metadata derived from ffprobe / client-side duration detection
219	  // when available; the UI falls back to the player's own duration
220	  // readout if it's null. Both fields are null for non-audio rows
221	  // and for audio rows uploaded before Supabase Storage was
222	  // configured — the blog post still renders, just text-only.
223	  audioUrl         String?
224	  audioDurationSec Int?
225	
226	  founder             Founder              @relation(fields: [founderId], references: [id])
227	  organization        Organization         @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
228	  auditEvents         AuditEvent[]
229	  deletionRequests    DeletionRequest[]
230	  // ── Provenance back-refs ─────────────────────────────────────────
231	  // Derived entities (Conclusions via the M:N join, Contradictions,
232	  // OpenQuestions, ResearchSuggestions directly) point back here so
233	  // the delete path can cascade-clean any artifact whose source has
234	  // been removed. `conclusionSources` is the many-to-many bridge —
235	  // the same text can be extracted from multiple uploads, and each
236	  // upload holds its own row in the bridge. The direct sourceUpload
237	  // relations on the other three entities are one-per-row: a
238	  // contradiction / open-question / research suggestion is an output
239	  // of exactly one ingestion run, even when it references claims from
240	  // multiple uploads.
241	  conclusionSources   ConclusionSource[]
242	  contradictions      Contradiction[]      @relation("ContradictionSourceUpload")
243	  openQuestions       OpenQuestion[]       @relation("OpenQuestionSourceUpload")
244	  researchSuggestions ResearchSuggestion[] @relation("ResearchSuggestionSourceUpload")
245	
246	  @@index([organizationId])
247	  @@index([founderId])
248	  @@index([publishedAt])
249	  @@index([deletedAt])
250	  @@index([visibility])
251	  // Dashboard filters by status constantly (ingested rows on the main
252	  // panel, failed/pending counts on the banner). A plain btree index
253	  // is enough — the org-scope is already covered by organizationId.
254	  @@index([status])
255	}
256	
257	// ── DeletionRequest ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
258	// Peer-to-peer "please delete this" workflow. A founder who doesn't own
259	// an upload but wants it removed opens a request against that upload;
260	// the owner reviews it (accept → soft-delete the upload, decline → log
261	// reason and keep). The requester can cancel their own request at any
262	// time before the owner responds.
263	//
264	// Enforcement:
265	//   * Requester.organizationId must equal Upload.organizationId (done
266	//     at route-handler level, not schema — cross-org requests are
267	//     structurally unreachable via UI anyway).
268	//   * Requester.id MUST NOT equal Upload.founderId (an owner deletes
269	//     their own upload directly via DELETE /api/uploads/:id; opening
270	//     a request against yourself is noise).
271	//   * At most one ACTIVE (status=pending) request per (upload, requester)
272	//     pair — the unique partial-index is added in the migration SQL.
273	
274	model DeletionRequest {
275	  id          String    @id @default(cuid())
276	  uploadId    String
277	  requesterId String
278	  status      String    @default("pending") // pending | accepted | declined | cancelled
279	  reason      String?
280	  decision    String? // optional note from the owner when accepting/declining
281	  createdAt   DateTime  @default(now())
282	  respondedAt DateTime?
283	
284	  upload    Upload  @relation(fields: [uploadId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
285	  requester Founder @relation(fields: [requesterId], references: [id])
286	
287	  @@index([uploadId])
288	  @@index([requesterId])
289	  @@index([status])
290	}
291	
292	// ── Conclusion — mirrors noosphere.models.Conclusion ───────────────────────
293	
294	model Conclusion {
295	  id             String @id @default(cuid())
296	  organizationId String
297	
298	  /// Stable id if synced from / produced for Noosphere
299	  noosphereId String? @unique
300	  text        String
301	
302	  // ── Dedup key ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
303	  // `normalizedText` is `LOWER(TRIM(REGEXP_REPLACE(text, '\s+', ' ')))`
304	  // computed once at ingest time. The ingest pipeline checks for an
305	  // existing Conclusion in the same org with a matching normalizedText;
306	  // if one exists, it adds the current upload as an ADDITIONAL source
307	  // via ConclusionSource rather than inserting a duplicate row. That's
308	  // what makes deletion correct: when two uploads produced the same
309	  // claim and we delete one, the conclusion stays — it's still
310	  // supported by the other source.
311	  //
312	  // Nullable so the column can be added to an existing DB without a
313	  // rewrite; the migration back-fills every existing row. Indexed
314	  // `(organizationId, normalizedText)` because every lookup is scoped
315	  // to a single org.
316	  normalizedText         String?
317	  confidenceTier         String // firm | founder | open (ConfidenceTier)
318	  rationale              String   @default("")
319	  supportingPrincipleIds String   @default("[]") // JSON string[]
320	  evidenceChainClaimIds  String   @default("[]")
321	  dissentClaimIds        String   @default("[]")
322	  confidence             Float    @default(0)
323	  topicHint              String   @default("") // UI filter / display
324	  createdAt              DateTime @default(now())
325	
326	  /// JSON-serialized float[] embedding produced by Noosphere's sentence
327	  /// encoder at ingest time. Null for conclusions ingested before this
328	  /// column was added; the semantic explorer backfills on demand.
329	  embeddingJson String?
330	
331	  attributedFounderId String?
332	  attributedFounder   Founder?                    @relation("ConclusionFounder", fields: [attributedFounderId], references: [id])
333	  organization        Organization                @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
334	  publicationReviews  PublicationReview[]
335	  sources             ConclusionSource[]
336	  deletionRequests    ConclusionDeletionRequest[]
337	  dashboardDismissals DashboardDismissal[]
338	
339	  @@index([organizationId])
340	  @@index([organizationId, normalizedText])
341	}
342	
343	/// Many-to-many bridge Conclusion ↔ Upload.
344	///
345	/// Every conclusion that came out of the ingest pipeline has at least
346	/// one row here tying it back to the upload it was extracted from.
347	/// When the SAME normalized text appears in multiple uploads, the
348	/// ingest pipeline reuses the existing Conclusion row and adds a new
349	/// ConclusionSource row per additional upload, so the upstream count
350	/// can drop from 2 → 1 → 0 as uploads are deleted.
351	///
352	/// Delete semantics (enforced by the app-side delete handlers, NOT by
353	/// FK cascade — we soft-delete uploads, so the FK's ON DELETE CASCADE
354	/// would not fire anyway):
355	///
356	///   * When an upload is soft-deleted, every ConclusionSource row
357	///     with that uploadId is hard-deleted.
358	///   * After that, any Conclusion with zero remaining rows is
359	///     hard-deleted too (the claim's last source is gone).
360	///   * Conclusions with sources from other uploads remain, just
361	///     with one fewer row.
362	///
363	/// The FK cascade below still serves as a safety net for the rare
364	/// case of a hard-delete (org takedown, privacy-law purge).
365	model ConclusionSource {
366	  conclusionId String
367	  uploadId     String
368	  createdAt    DateTime @default(now())
369	
370	  conclusion Conclusion @relation(fields: [conclusionId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
371	  upload     Upload     @relation(fields: [uploadId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
372	
373	  @@id([conclusionId, uploadId])
374	  @@index([conclusionId])
375	  @@index([uploadId])
376	}
377	
378	// ── Publication (SP10) — review queue + versioned public snapshots ───────────
379	
380	/// Internal gate before a conclusion may appear on the public site.
381	model PublicationReview {
382	  id             String @id @default(cuid())
383	  organizationId String
384	  conclusionId   String
385	
386	  /// queued | in_review | needs_revision | declined | published
387	  status        String @default("queued")
388	  /// JSON: { metaAnalysisOk, adversarialEngagedOk, clarityOk, noLeakageOk }
389	  checklistJson String @default("{}")
390	  reviewerNotes String @default("")
391	  declineReason String @default("")
392	  revisionAsk   String @default("")
393	
394	  reviewerFounderId String?
395	  createdAt         DateTime @default(now())
396	  updatedAt         DateTime @updatedAt
397	
398	  organization Organization @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
399	  target       Conclusion   @relation(fields: [conclusionId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
400	  reviewer     Founder?     @relation("PublicationReviewer", fields: [reviewerFounderId], references: [id])
401	
402	  @@index([organizationId])
403	  @@index([conclusionId])
404	  @@index([status])
405	}
406	
407	/// Immutable-ish public snapshot (new row per material revision).
408	model PublishedConclusion {
409	  id                 String @id @default(cuid())
410	  organizationId     String
411	  sourceConclusionId String
412	
413	  slug    String
414	  version Int    @default(1)
415	
416	  /// Headline discounted confidence (SP05); stated founder/model confidence alongside in JSON.
417	  discountedConfidence      Float
418	  statedConfidence          Float  @default(0)
419	  calibrationDiscountReason String @default("")
420	
421	  /// Full page payload: evidence, exit conditions, adversarial, voice map, timeline, citations.
422	  payloadJson String @default("{}")
423	
424	  /// Zenodo DOI for this revision (minted on first publish of revision).
425	  doi            String @default("")
426	  zenodoRecordId String @default("")
427	
428	  publishedAt DateTime @default(now())
429	
430	  organization Organization     @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
431	  responses    PublicResponse[]
432	
433	  @@unique([slug, version])
434	  @@index([organizationId])
435	  @@index([slug])
436	}
437	
438	/// Structured reader responses — moderation queue (SP10).
439	model PublicResponse {
440	  id             String @id @default(cuid())
441	  organizationId String
442	
443	  publishedConclusionId String
444	
445	  /// counter_evidence | counter_argument | clarification | agreement_extension
446	  kind           String
447	  body           String
448	  citationUrl    String  @default("")
449	  submitterEmail String  @default("")
450	  orcid          String  @default("")
451	  pseudonymous   Boolean @default(false)
452	
453	  /// pending | approved | rejected | engaged
454	  status        String @default("pending")
455	  moderatorNote String @default("")
456	
457	  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
458	
459	  organization Organization        @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
460	  published    PublishedConclusion @relation(fields: [publishedConclusionId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
461	
462	  @@index([organizationId])
463	  @@index([publishedConclusionId])
464	  @@index([status])
465	}
466	
467	// ── ContradictionFinding + scores — mirrors pipeline outputs ────────────────
468	
469	model Contradiction {
470	  id             String @id @default(cuid())
471	  organizationId String
472	
473	  claimAId     String
474	  claimBId     String
475	  severity     Float
476	  sixLayerJson String?
477	  narrative    String   @default("")
478	  createdAt    DateTime @default(now())
479	
480	  // ── Source tracking ───────────────────────────────────────────────
481	  // The upload whose ingestion run produced this contradiction row.
482	  // Nullable so the column can be backfilled from `noosphereId` patterns
483	  // on existing rows; the ingest pipeline sets it on every new row.
484	  // When the source upload is deleted the contradiction is hard-deleted
485	  // in the same transaction (see src/app/api/upload/[id]/delete/route.ts);
486	  // cross-upload contradictions (where claimA and claimB come from
487	  // different uploads) additionally die if EITHER of their referenced
488	  // Conclusions has already been orphan-deleted.
489	  sourceUploadId String?
490	  sourceUpload   Upload? @relation("ContradictionSourceUpload", fields: [sourceUploadId], references: [id])
491	
492	  // ── Resolution tracking ────────────────────────────────────────────
493	  // A contradiction starts "active". A founder can resolve it
494	  // (acknowledged + addressed with a note) or dismiss it (false
495	  // positive). Non-active rows are hidden from the main view by
496	  // default but preserved for audit.
497	  status       String    @default("active") // active | resolved | dismissed
498	  resolution   String?
499	  resolvedById String?
500	  resolvedAt   DateTime?
501	  resolvedBy   Founder?  @relation("ContradictionResolver", fields: [resolvedById], references: [id])
502	
503	  organization Organization @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
504	
505	  @@index([organizationId])
506	  @@index([sourceUploadId])
507	  @@index([status])
508	}
509	
510	// ── ConclusionDeletionRequest ─────────────────────────────────────────
511	// Peer-to-peer "please delete this conclusion" workflow, mirroring
512	// DeletionRequest for uploads. A founder opens a request; the
513	// conclusion's attributed founder (or an admin) accepts or declines.
514	// On accept: hard-delete the Conclusion (and its ConclusionSource rows
515	// via FK cascade) so it's purged from the model.
516	
517	model ConclusionDeletionRequest {
518	  id           String    @id @default(cuid())
519	  conclusionId String
520	  requesterId  String
521	  status       String    @default("pending") // pending | accepted | declined | cancelled
522	  reason       String?
523	  decision     String?
524	  createdAt    DateTime  @default(now())
525	  respondedAt  DateTime?
526	
527	  conclusion Conclusion @relation(fields: [conclusionId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
528	  requester  Founder    @relation(fields: [requesterId], references: [id])
529	
530	  @@index([conclusionId])
531	  @@index([requesterId])
532	  @@index([status])
533	}
534	
535	// ── DashboardDismissal ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
536	// Per-founder UI preference. Dismissing a conclusion from the dashboard
537	// hides it from *this* founder's homepage only — other founders in the
538	// same org still see it. Not a deletion.
539	
540	model DashboardDismissal {
541	  id           String   @id @default(cuid())
542	  founderId    String
543	  conclusionId String
544	  createdAt    DateTime @default(now())
545	
546	  founder    Founder    @relation(fields: [founderId], references: [id])
547	  conclusion Conclusion @relation(fields: [conclusionId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
548	
549	  @@unique([founderId, conclusionId])
550	  @@index([founderId])
551	  @@index([conclusionId])
552	}
553	
554	// ── DriftEvent — mirrors noosphere.models.DriftEvent ───────────────────────
555	
556	model DriftEvent {
557	  id             String @id @default(cuid())
558	  organizationId String
559	
560	  noosphereId                 String?  @unique
561	  targetId                    String
562	  targetKind                  String   @default("principle")
563	  episodeId                   String   @default("")
564	  observedAt                  DateTime
565	  driftScore                  Float    @default(0)
566	  notes                       String   @default("")
567	  claimSequenceIdsJson        String   @default("[]")
568	  naturalLanguageSummary      String   @default("")
569	  earliestInconsistentClaimId String   @default("")
570	  authorTopicKey              String   @default("")
571	  topicId                     String   @default("")
572	
573	  organization Organization @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
574	
575	  @@index([organizationId])
576	}
577	
578	// ── ResearchSuggestion — mirrors noosphere.models.ResearchSuggestion ───────
579	
580	model ResearchSuggestion {
581	  id             String @id @default(cuid())
582	  organizationId String
583	
584	  noosphereId  String?  @unique
585	  title        String
586	  summary      String   @default("")
587	  rationale    String   @default("")
588	  readingUris  String   @default("[]") // JSON string[]
589	  sessionLabel String   @default("")
590	  createdAt    DateTime @default(now())
591	
592	  // ── Source tracking ───────────────────────────────────────────────
593	  // The upload whose ingestion run generated this suggestion. Unlike
594	  // Conclusion, ResearchSuggestion is NOT deduplicated across uploads
595	  // — if two uploads both happen to prompt the same "read X" suggestion
596	  // they each get their own row. When the source upload is deleted
597	  // the suggestion goes with it.
598	  sourceUploadId String?
599	  sourceUpload   Upload? @relation("ResearchSuggestionSourceUpload", fields: [sourceUploadId], references: [id])
600	
601	  suggestedForFounderId String?
602	  suggestedForFounder   Founder?     @relation(fields: [suggestedForFounderId], references: [id])
603	  organization          Organization @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
604	
605	  @@index([organizationId])
606	  @@index([sourceUploadId])
607	}
608	
609	// ── ReviewItem — mirrors noosphere.models.ReviewItem (+ portal resolution) ─
610	
611	model ReviewItem {
612	  id             String @id @default(cuid())
613	  organizationId String
614	
615	  noosphereId       String?   @unique
616	  claimAId          String
617	  claimBId          String
618	  reason            String    @default("")
619	  layerVerdictsJson String    @default("{}") // dict str -> str
620	  severity          Float     @default(0)
621	  status            String    @default("open") // open | done | dismissed
622	  aggregatorVerdict String?
623	  priorScoresJson   String?
624	  humanVerdict      String?
625	  humanOverrule     Boolean   @default(false)
626	  resolutionNote    String    @default("")
627	  createdAt         DateTime  @default(now())
628	  resolvedAt        DateTime?
629	
630	  resolvedByFounderId String?
631	  resolvedByFounder   Founder?     @relation("ReviewResolver", fields: [resolvedByFounderId], references: [id])
632	  organization        Organization @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
633	
634	  @@index([organizationId])
635	}
636	
637	// ── OpenQuestionCandidate — mirrors conclusions.OpenQuestionCandidate ───────
638	
639	model OpenQuestion {
640	  id             String @id @default(cuid())
641	  organizationId String
642	
643	  noosphereId              String?  @unique
644	  summary                  String
645	  claimAId                 String
646	  claimBId                 String
647	  unresolvedReason         String   @default("")
648	  layerDisagreementSummary String   @default("")
649	  createdAt                DateTime @default(now())
650	
651	  // ── Source tracking ───────────────────────────────────────────────
652	  // The upload whose ingestion produced this open question. Cascaded
653	  // with the upload's soft-delete; also disappears if either of its
654	  // referenced Conclusions has been orphan-deleted (a question about
655	  // a claim that no longer exists in the Codex has no meaning).
656	  sourceUploadId String?
657	  sourceUpload   Upload? @relation("OpenQuestionSourceUpload", fields: [sourceUploadId], references: [id])
658	
659	  organization Organization @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
660	
661	  @@index([organizationId])
662	  @@index([sourceUploadId])
663	}
664	
665	model AuditEvent {
666	  id             String   @id @default(cuid())
667	  organizationId String
668	  founderId      String
669	  uploadId       String?
670	  action         String
671	  detail         String?
672	  createdAt      DateTime @default(now())
673	
674	  founder      Founder      @relation(fields: [founderId], references: [id])
675	  organization Organization @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
676	  upload       Upload?      @relation(fields: [uploadId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)
677	
678	  @@index([organizationId])
679	}
680	
681	// ── API keys for machine-to-machine auth (Dialectic auto-sync, scripts, etc.) ─
682	//
683	// Store the *hash* of the key, never the plaintext. The plaintext is returned
684	// once at creation time. Revocation = set `revokedAt`. The key plaintext
685	// should look like `tcx_<founderId>_<64-hex-random>` so the client can send
686	// the founderId bits without a DB lookup for routing, and the random tail is
687	// the secret. `authenticateApiKey()` in src/lib/apiKeyAuth.ts implements
688	// the verification (bcrypt compare against `keyHash`).
689	model ApiKey {
690	  id             String @id @default(cuid())
691	  organizationId String
692	  /// Founder the key acts on behalf of — uploads are attributed to them.
693	  founderId      String
694	
695	  /// Human label shown in the management UI (e.g. "Dialectic on laptop").
696	  label   String
697	  /// Short prefix of the plaintext (first 12 chars) — shown in UI for identification.
698	  prefix  String
699	  /// bcrypt hash of the full plaintext key. Never store plaintext.
700	  keyHash String
701	  /// Comma-separated scopes (e.g. "upload:write"). Empty = full founder scope.
702	  scopes  String @default("")
703	
704	  createdAt  DateTime  @default(now())
705	  lastUsedAt DateTime?
706	  revokedAt  DateTime?
707	
708	  founder      Founder      @relation(fields: [founderId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
709	  organization Organization @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
710	
711	  @@index([organizationId])
712	  @@index([founderId])
713	  @@index([prefix])
714	}

1 import Link from "next/link"; 2 import { revalidatePath } from "next/cache"; 3 import { Prisma } from "@prisma/client"; 4 import ConfidenceTierSigil from "@/components/ConfidenceTierSigil"; 5 import SculptureBackdrop from "@/components/SculptureBackdrop"; 6 import AutoProcessStatusBanner from "@/components/AutoProcessStatusBanner"; 7 import PublishToggle from "@/components/PublishToggle"; 8 import UploadStatusBadge from "@/components/UploadStatusBadge"; 9 import UploadRowDetail from "@/components/UploadRowDetail"; 10 import { db } from "@/lib/db"; 11 import { fetchDecayRecords } from "@/lib/api/round3"; 12 import { requireTenantContext } from "@/lib/tenant"; 13 14 /** 15 * Dashboard — landing page after login. 16 * 17 * Patron sculpture: **Sisyphus**, rendered huge and dim on the right 18 * side of the page. The daily act of returning to the Codex — scanning 19 * newly-synthesized conclusions, picking up where yesterday's 20 * deliberation left off, discovering the contradictions that drifted 21 * overnight — is the boulder at firm-memory scale. 1	"""
2	Process Theseus Codex uploads locally.
3	
4	Story
5	-----
6	The Codex web app (on Vercel) accepts file uploads into its Postgres
7	database but cannot run the Noosphere Python pipeline in that runtime —
8	Vercel's serverless Node has no Python interpreter. Uploads land as
9	``queued_offline`` with their ``textContent`` sitting in the DB until
10	someone runs this command on a machine that DOES have Noosphere.
11	
12	What it does
13	------------
14	Given an Upload id, this module:
15	
16	1. Connects to the Codex's Postgres (the shared Supabase instance — same
17	   DB, different tables than Noosphere would normally write to).
18	2. Reads the ``Upload`` row, pulls ``textContent``.
19	3. Extracts atomic claims from the text. Two extraction modes:
20	     - default / naive: rule-based sentence splitter that keeps only
21	       assertoric-looking sentences (filters questions, fragments, mere
22	       listings). Zero dependencies beyond the standard library, works
23	       without an LLM API key.
24	     - ``--with-llm``: delegates to the registered ``extract_claims``
25	       method (the Noosphere LLM pipeline). Higher quality, requires
26	       ``ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`` or ``OPENAI_API_KEY`` via noosphere.config.
27	4. Writes each extracted claim back into the Codex's ``Conclusion``
28	   table (tier=``open``, confidence=0.5) so it appears in the Codex web
29	   UI immediately.
30	5. Updates the Upload row to ``status='ingested'`` and sets
31	   ``claimsCount`` so the dashboard counters reflect the work.
32	6. Writes an ``AuditEvent`` row for traceability.
33	
34	Why write to the Codex schema directly instead of Noosphere's own
35	-----------------------------------------------------------------
36	The goal of this command is "user uploaded a file, user sees something
37	in the Codex UI after running Noosphere locally". Writing to Noosphere's
38	separate tables would require the Codex UI to also query those tables
39	(the existing Python-spawn bridges do this, but they don't work on
40	Vercel either). Writing directly to the Codex's ``Conclusion`` table
41	gives the end-to-end "I uploaded, I can see analysis" flow without any
42	additional infrastructure. Full Noosphere-side artifact/chunk/claim
43	storage can be added later via ``--also-write-noosphere``.
44	"""
45	
46	from __future__ import annotations
47	
48	import json
49	import os
50	import re
51	import sys
52	import uuid
53	from dataclasses import dataclass
54	from datetime import datetime, timezone
55	from typing import Optional
56	
57	try:
58	    import psycopg2
59	    import psycopg2.extras
60	except ImportError as e:  # pragma: no cover
61	    raise RuntimeError(
62	        "psycopg2 is required for the Codex bridge. "
63	        "Install with: pip install psycopg2-binary"
64	    ) from e
65	
66	
67	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
68	# SQLite shim — test-only codex connection.
69	#
70	# Prod always points at Supabase / Postgres. The ingest test suite spins up an
71	# in-process SQLite DB seeded from ``tests/fixtures/minimal_codex_schema.sql``
72	# so the pipeline SQL can be exercised without a running Postgres. The shim
73	# below is the cheapest adapter that lets the (otherwise psycopg2-shaped)
74	# pipeline run unchanged under either backend: translate ``%s`` → ``?``,
75	# yield dict-shaped rows, and expose the handful of cursor/connection methods
76	# the pipeline actually calls.
77	#
78	# Triggered by a ``sqlite://<path>`` URL; anything else goes to psycopg2.
79	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
80	
81	
82	class _SqliteCodexCursor:
83	    """Translate psycopg2-style calls to sqlite3 calls and return dict rows."""
84	
85	    def __init__(self, cur):
86	        self._cur = cur
87	
88	    @staticmethod
89	    def _translate(query: str) -> str:
90	        return query.replace("%s", "?")
91	
92	    def execute(self, query, params=()):
93	        self._cur.execute(self._translate(query), params or ())
94	        return self
95	
96	    def executemany(self, query, seq):
97	        self._cur.executemany(self._translate(query), seq)
98	        return self
99	
100	    def fetchone(self):
101	        row = self._cur.fetchone()
102	        return dict(row) if row is not None else None
103	
104	    def fetchall(self):
105	        return [dict(r) for r in self._cur.fetchall()]
106	
107	    def __iter__(self):
108	        for row in self._cur:
109	            yield dict(row)
110	
111	    @property
112	    def rowcount(self):
113	        return self._cur.rowcount
114	
115	    def close(self):
116	        self._cur.close()
117	
118	
119	class _SqliteCodexConn:
120	    def __init__(self, conn):
121	        self._conn = conn
122	
123	    def cursor(self, *_args, **_kwargs):
124	        return _SqliteCodexCursor(self._conn.cursor())
125	
126	    def commit(self):
127	        self._conn.commit()
128	
129	    def rollback(self):
130	        self._conn.rollback()
131	
132	    def close(self):
133	        self._conn.close()
134	
135	
136	def _open_codex_connection(url: str):
137	    """Open a Codex DB connection. ``sqlite://<path>`` triggers the test
138	    shim; anything else is treated as a Postgres URL for psycopg2."""
139	    if url.startswith("sqlite://"):
140	        import sqlite3
141	
142	        path = url[len("sqlite://"):]
143	        conn = sqlite3.connect(path)
144	        conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
145	        return _SqliteCodexConn(conn)
146	    return psycopg2.connect(url)
147	
148	
149	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
150	# Postgres-safe sanitization.
151	#
152	# Postgres's UTF-8 enforcement rejects strings containing the NUL
153	# byte (``\u0000``) with "invalid byte sequence for encoding "UTF8":
154	# 0x00". PDF extraction, DOCX extraction, and even LLM output
155	# occasionally produce them. Every string that's about to be inserted
156	# into the Codex DB flows through ``_db_safe`` first so one bad
157	# byte doesn't nuke the whole ingest.
158	#
159	# We also strip other C0 control chars (except tab/newline/CR) and
160	# lone UTF-16 surrogate halves, which behave the same way on some
161	# server collations.
162	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
163	
164	# C0 controls minus \t (\x09), \n (\x0a), \r (\x0d). Also nuke BOM.
165	_DB_UNSAFE = re.compile(r"[\x00-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\ufeff]")
166	_DB_SURROGATE = re.compile(r"[\ud800-\udfff]")
167	
168	
169	def _db_safe(s: Optional[str], *, cap: Optional[int] = None) -> str:
170	    """Scrub for Postgres and optionally cap length.
171	
172	    ``None`` / non-string inputs return ``""`` so callers can unconditionally
173	    pass field values without null-checking first.
174	    """
175	    if s is None:
176	        return ""
177	    text = str(s)
178	    text = _DB_UNSAFE.sub("", text)
179	    text = _DB_SURROGATE.sub("", text)
180	    if cap is not None and len(text) > cap:
181	        text = text[:cap]
182	    return text
183	
184	
185	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
186	# Conclusion dedup — normalised text form.
187	#
188	# The Codex DB has a `Conclusion.normalizedText` column populated by this
189	# helper and matched by a `(organizationId, normalizedText)` index. Any
190	# time this pipeline is about to write a new Conclusion we first look up
191	# an existing row with the same normalised key in the same org; if one
192	# exists we skip the INSERT and add this upload as an extra source via
193	# `ConclusionSource`. That's what makes deletion work correctly — a
194	# Conclusion whose one-and-only source is removed gets orphan-deleted,
195	# while a Conclusion supported by several uploads stays in place with
196	# the surviving uploads as its remaining sources.
197	#
198	# The formula (lower + strip + collapse whitespace) matches the SQL
199	# backfill in
200	# `prisma/migrations/20260420000000_conclusion_sources_and_derived_cascade`,
201	# so pre-existing rows and freshly-inserted rows share the same key.
202	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
203	
204	_WS_COLLAPSE_RE = re.compile(r"\s+")
205	
206	
207	def _normalize_claim_text(text: str) -> str:
208	    """Return the dedup key for a claim. Empty input → empty key."""
209	    if not text:
210	        return ""
211	    return _WS_COLLAPSE_RE.sub(" ", text).strip().lower()
212	
213	
214	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
215	# Naive claim extraction — works without an LLM. Conservative: keeps only
216	# sentences that look like first-person / declarative assertions, filters
217	# questions and fragments. Good enough for a first-pass Codex display.
218	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
219	
220	
221	# Sentence-level tokens ending with a period / exclamation / line break.
222	# Non-greedy so we don't over-fuse sentences across paragraph breaks.
223	_SENT_RE = re.compile(r"[^.!?\n]+[.!?]+|[^.!?\n]+(?=\n|$)", re.MULTILINE)
224	
225	# Heuristic filters for sentences we SHOULDN'T promote to claims.
226	_QUESTION_RE = re.compile(r"\?\s*$")
227	_LIST_BULLET_RE = re.compile(r"^\s*[-*•\d]+[\.\)]?\s+")
228	_URL_RE = re.compile(r"https?://|www\.")
229	_HEADER_RE = re.compile(r"^#+\s|^={3,}\s*$|^-{3,}\s*$")
230	
231	
232	@dataclass
233	class NaiveClaim:
234	    text: str
235	    claim_type: str = "empirical"  # conservative default
236	
237	
238	def naive_extract_claims(text: str, *, max_claims: int = 40) -> list[NaiveClaim]:
239	    """
240	    Sentence-split a blob of text and keep only sentences that look like
241	    substantive claims:
242	      * length ≥ 40 chars (filters "Yes.", "Ok.", lone bullets)
243	      * length ≤ 400 chars (filters run-on paragraphs that aren't atomic)
244	      * not a question (``?`` at end)
245	      * not a list bullet / markdown header
246	      * contains at least one verb-like word (``is|are|was|were|should|must|can|may|will``)
247	        OR a contextual connector (``because|therefore|given|since``)
248	
249	    Returns at most ``max_claims`` so a 50-page transcript doesn't blow
250	    up the Codex with thousands of rows.
251	    """
252	    out: list[NaiveClaim] = []
253	    cleaned = _HEADER_RE.sub("", text)
254	    # Normalise whitespace so sentence splitting doesn't fragment on line
255	    # wraps inside a paragraph. We preserve paragraph breaks (double-newline)
256	    # because they are a useful signal for separating assertions; we just
257	    # rejoin line wraps WITHIN a paragraph into spaces.
258	    cleaned = re.sub(r"(?<!\n)\n(?!\n)", " ", cleaned)
259	    cleaned = re.sub(r"[ \t]+", " ", cleaned)
260	    # Walk sentences.
261	    for match in _SENT_RE.finditer(cleaned):
262	        raw = match.group(0).strip()
263	        if not raw:
264	            continue
265	        if len(raw) < 40 or len(raw) > 400:
266	            continue
267	        if _QUESTION_RE.search(raw):
268	            continue
269	        if _LIST_BULLET_RE.match(raw):
270	            # Strip the bullet and re-check length.
271	            stripped = _LIST_BULLET_RE.sub("", raw).strip()
272	            if len(stripped) < 40 or len(stripped) > 400:
273	                continue
274	            raw = stripped
275	        if _URL_RE.search(raw):
276	            # Drop sentences that are mostly URLs — they're rarely claims.
277	            continue
278	        lower = raw.lower()
279	        has_verb = bool(
280	            re.search(
281	                r"\b(is|are|was|were|should|must|can|may|will|would|do|does|did|have|has|had|seems|appears|means|implies|suggests|shows|proves|causes|requires|matters)\b",
282	                lower,
283	            )
284	        )
285	        has_connector = bool(
286	            re.search(r"\b(because|therefore|thus|hence|since|given that)\b", lower)
287	        )
288	        if not (has_verb or has_connector):
289	            continue
290	        # Classify: rough taxonomy based on vocabulary.
291	        claim_type = "empirical"
292	        if re.search(r"\b(should|must|ought|right|wrong|better|worse|good|bad)\b", lower):
293	            claim_type = "normative"
294	        elif re.search(
295	            r"\b(method|framework|approach|process|procedure|methodology)\b", lower
296	        ):
297	            claim_type = "methodological"
298	        elif re.search(r"\b(will|won't|shall|going to|expect|predict)\b", lower):
299	            claim_type = "predictive"
300	        out.append(NaiveClaim(text=raw, claim_type=claim_type))
301	        if len(out) >= max_claims:
302	            break
303	    return out
304

306	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
307	# LLM-backed extraction — uses Noosphere's registered ``extract_claims`` method.
308	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
309	
310	
311	def llm_extract_claims(
312	    text: str,
313	    *,
314	    chunk_char_size: int = 3500,
315	    max_chunks: int = 10,
316	    upload_id: str = "upload",
317	) -> list[NaiveClaim]:
318	    """LLM-backed extraction via Noosphere's ``extract_claims`` method.
319	
320	    Raises if the LLM is unconfigured — the caller is expected to have
321	    passed --with-llm deliberately.
322	    """
323	    from noosphere.methods.extract_claims import (
324	        ExtractClaimsInput,
325	        extract_claims,
326	    )
327	
328	    # Chunk the text so no single LLM call blows past the context budget.
329	    # Paragraph splits preferred; fall back to hard-cut if a paragraph is
330	    # itself huge.
331	    raw_paras = re.split(r"\n\s*\n+", text)
332	    chunks: list[str] = []
333	    buf: list[str] = []
334	    size = 0
335	    for p in raw_paras:
336	        if size + len(p) > chunk_char_size and buf:
337	            chunks.append("\n\n".join(buf))
338	            buf = []
339	            size = 0
340	        if len(p) > chunk_char_size:
341	            # Hard-split the monster paragraph.
342	            for i in range(0, len(p), chunk_char_size):
343	                chunks.append(p[i : i + chunk_char_size])
344	        else:
345	            buf.append(p)
346	            size += len(p) + 2
347	    if buf:
348	        chunks.append("\n\n".join(buf))
349	
350	    chunks = chunks[:max_chunks]
351	
352	    out: list[NaiveClaim] = []
353	    for i, chunk in enumerate(chunks):
354	        result = extract_claims(
355	            ExtractClaimsInput(
356	                chunk_text=chunk,
357	                chunk_id=f"{upload_id}_chunk_{i}",
358	                episode_id=upload_id,
359	                speaker_name="founder",
360	                speaker_role="founder",
361	            )
362	        )
363	        for c in result.claims:
364	            if not c.text.strip():
365	                continue
366	            # Drop claims the LLM flagged as external (interview prompts,
367	            # counter-positions, quoted views) so they never land in the
368	            # founder's Conclusion canon. This is defense-in-depth: the
369	            # extractor already labels them, the bridge refuses to
370	            # propagate them.
371	            if getattr(c, "is_author_assertion", True) is False:
372	                continue
373	            out.append(NaiveClaim(text=c.text.strip(), claim_type=c.claim_type))
374	    return out
375

378	# Core pipeline — connects to Codex Postgres, reads upload, writes results.
379	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
380

383	class IngestFromCodexResult:
384	    upload_id: str
385	    title: str
386	    num_claims_extracted: int
387	    num_conclusions_written: int

389	    num_open_questions_written: int

391	    dry_run: bool
392	    mode: str  # "naive" | "llm"

395	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
396	# Contradiction detection — naive (always runs) + LLM-augmented (optional).
397	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
398	
399	
400	@dataclass
401	class DetectedContradiction:
402	    """A contradicting claim pair. `a_idx` / `b_idx` refer to indices in
403	    the claim list we just extracted; if one side is an existing firm
404	    Conclusion (cross-upload contradiction) we use `b_existing_id` instead
405	    of `b_idx`."""
406	    a_idx: int
407	    b_idx: int | None  # None when b is an existing firm Conclusion
408	    b_existing_id: str | None  # Conclusion.id when b is from the firm corpus
409	    severity: float   # 0..1
410	    narrative: str
411	    source: str       # "heuristic" | "llm"
412	
413	
414	_NEGATION_TOKENS = re.compile(
415	    r"\b(not|never|no|none|nothing|nobody|nowhere|n'?t|cannot|can'?t|won'?t|"
416	    r"shouldn'?t|wouldn'?t|couldn'?t|isn'?t|aren'?t|wasn'?t|weren'?t|"
417	    r"doesn'?t|didn'?t|don'?t|haven'?t|hasn'?t|hadn'?t)\b",
418	    flags=re.IGNORECASE,
419	)
420	_STOPWORDS = {
421	    "the", "a", "an", "is", "are", "was", "were", "be", "been", "being",
422	    "and", "or", "but", "if", "then", "of", "in", "on", "at", "to", "for",
423	    "by", "with", "from", "as", "that", "this", "these", "those", "it",
424	    "its", "we", "our", "you", "they", "them", "their", "i", "me", "my",
425	    "he", "she", "his", "her", "has", "have", "had", "do", "does", "did",
426	    "will", "would", "should", "could", "can", "may", "must", "ought",
427	    "so", "than", "such", "some", "any", "all", "every", "each", "both",
428	    "more", "most", "much", "many", "few",
429	}
430	
431	
432	def _content_tokens(text: str) -> set[str]:
433	    toks = re.findall(r"\b[a-z][a-z']+\b", text.lower())
434	    return {t for t in toks if t not in _STOPWORDS and len(t) >= 4}
435	
436	
437	def _has_negation(text: str) -> bool:
438	    return bool(_NEGATION_TOKENS.search(text))
439	
440	
441	def naive_detect_contradictions(
442	    claims: list[NaiveClaim],
443	) -> list[DetectedContradiction]:
444	    """
445	    Heuristic pairwise contradiction scan on the new claims. Flags pairs
446	    where:
447	      (1) their content vocabulary overlaps significantly (same subject), AND
448	      (2) exactly one contains a negation marker (opposite polarity).
449	
450	    This catches the obvious "X should always …" vs "X should not …" case
451	    without needing an NLI model. Recall is modest; precision is decent
452	    enough that the flagged pairs are usually worth a human's attention.
453	    """
454	    out: list[DetectedContradiction] = []
455	    tok_sets = [_content_tokens(c.text) for c in claims]
456	    neg_flags = [_has_negation(c.text) for c in claims]
457	    for i in range(len(claims)):
458	        for j in range(i + 1, len(claims)):
459	            if neg_flags[i] == neg_flags[j]:
460	                continue  # both affirmative or both negative — no polarity flip
461	            if not tok_sets[i] or not tok_sets[j]:
462	                continue
463	            shared = tok_sets[i] & tok_sets[j]
464	            # Require meaningful overlap — otherwise it's just two unrelated
465	            # sentences that happen to have different polarity. A Jaccard
466	            # of 0.35 on content tokens is a useful middle ground.
467	            smaller = min(len(tok_sets[i]), len(tok_sets[j]))
468	            if smaller == 0 or (len(shared) / smaller) < 0.35:
469	                continue
470	            # Severity proxy: how much do they overlap? More overlap = more
471	            # directly contradictory.
472	            overlap_ratio = len(shared) / smaller
473	            severity = min(0.9, 0.4 + overlap_ratio * 0.5)
474	            out.append(
475	                DetectedContradiction(
476	                    a_idx=i,
477	                    b_idx=j,
478	                    b_existing_id=None,
479	                    severity=severity,
480	                    narrative=(
481	                        f"Heuristic: shared vocabulary {sorted(shared)[:6]} "
482	                        f"but opposite polarity."
483	                    ),
484	                    source="heuristic",
485	                )
486	            )
487	    return out
488

490	def llm_detect_contradictions_and_questions(
491	    claims: list[NaiveClaim],
492	    firm_conclusions: list[tuple[str, str]],  # (id, text) of existing firm claims
493	    *,
494	    upload_title: str,
495	) -> dict:
496	    """
497	    Single LLM call that does all three analysis tasks at once —
498	    contradictions, open questions, research suggestions. Putting them in
499	    one prompt is:
500	      (a) cheaper (single context load);
501	      (b) more coherent — the questions are generated in light of the
502	          contradictions the LLM just identified, instead of being
503	          independently invented.
504	
505	    Returns a dict shaped like:
506	      {
507	        "contradictions": [{"a": int, "b": int, "severity": float, "narrative": str}],
508	        "cross_contradictions": [{"a": int, "existing_id": str, "severity": float, "narrative": str}],
509	        "open_questions": [{"summary": str, "unresolved_reason": str, "a": int, "b": int|None}],
510	        "research_suggestions": [{"title": str, "summary": str, "rationale": str}],
511	      }
512	    Missing keys are backfilled to empty lists so the caller never
513	    explodes on a partial response.
514	
515	    Raises on LLM failure so the caller can decide whether to fall back
516	    to heuristic-only mode.
517	    """
518	    from noosphere.llm import llm_client_from_settings
519	
520	    llm = llm_client_from_settings()
521	
522	    new_lines = "\n".join(
523	        f"  [{i}] {c.text}" for i, c in enumerate(claims)
524	    )
525	    firm_lines = (
526	        "\n".join(f"  <{fid[:10]}> {txt[:240]}" for fid, txt in firm_conclusions[:40])
527	        if firm_conclusions
528	        else "  (none yet — this is the first upload for this firm.)"
529	    )
530	
531	    system = (
532	        "You are an analyst helping a firm surface contradictions, open "
533	        "questions, and research directions from a newly uploaded document. "
534	        "Answer ONLY in JSON matching the schema below. Be conservative — "
535	        "only flag genuine contradictions, not mere topical overlap. Only "
536	        "propose questions a founder could actually research. Never "
537	        "fabricate evidence."
538	    )
539	    user = f"""New document: "{upload_title}"
540	
541	CLAIMS extracted from this document (indices in brackets):
542	{new_lines}
543	
544	FIRM'S EXISTING CONCLUSIONS (for cross-upload contradiction detection):
545	{firm_lines}
546	
547	Return a single JSON object with these keys:
548	{{
549	  "contradictions": [
550	    {{"a": <idx>, "b": <idx>, "severity": <0..1>, "narrative": "<≤200 chars, why they conflict>"}}
551	  ],
552	  "cross_contradictions": [
553	    {{"a": <idx>, "existing_id": "<existing firm claim id prefix>", "severity": <0..1>, "narrative": "<≤200 chars>"}}
554	  ],
555	  "open_questions": [
556	    {{"summary": "<≤180 chars, a concrete question>", "unresolved_reason": "<why unresolved>", "a": <idx or null>, "b": <idx or null>}}
557	  ],
558	  "research_suggestions": [
559	    {{"title": "<short title>", "summary": "<one sentence>", "rationale": "<why this matters>"}}
560	  ]
561	}}
562	
563	Rules:
564	- 0-5 contradictions, 0-3 cross_contradictions, 3-6 open_questions, 2-4 research_suggestions.
565	- If no real contradictions exist, return [] for that list. Do not pad.
566	- `a` and `b` must be valid indices into the CLAIMS list above.
567	- Output ONLY the JSON object, no prose."""
568	
569	    raw = llm.complete(system=system, user=user, max_tokens=2500)
570	    m = re.search(r"\{.*\}", raw, re.DOTALL)
571	    if not m:
572	        raise RuntimeError(f"LLM returned non-JSON: {raw[:200]}")
573	    payload = json.loads(m.group(0))
574	    return {
575	        "contradictions": payload.get("contradictions", []) or [],
576	        "cross_contradictions": payload.get("cross_contradictions", []) or [],
577	        "open_questions": payload.get("open_questions", []) or [],
578	        "research_suggestions": payload.get("research_suggestions", []) or [],
579	    }
580

582	def _resolve_codex_db_url(explicit: Optional[str]) -> str:
583	    if explicit:
584	        return explicit
585	    for env in (
586	        "THESEUS_CODEX_DATABASE_URL",
587	        "CODEX_DATABASE_URL",
588	        "DIRECT_URL",
589	        "DATABASE_URL",
590	    ):
591	        val = os.environ.get(env, "").strip()
592	        if val:
593	            return val
594	    raise RuntimeError(
595	        "No Codex Postgres URL found. Pass --codex-db-url, or export "
596	        "THESEUS_CODEX_DATABASE_URL / CODEX_DATABASE_URL / DIRECT_URL / "
597	        "DATABASE_URL. The Supabase DIRECT_URL (port 5432, not the 6543 "
598	        "pooler) is preferred — the pooler can strip advisory locks some "
599	        "ORMs rely on."
600	    )
601	
602	
603	def _short_cuid_like() -> str:
604	    """Simple unique id for Conclusion / AuditEvent rows. Prisma normally
605	    uses cuid() but the DB only enforces uniqueness, so a uuid4-derived
606	    id works fine and keeps this file Python-only (no cuid dep)."""
607	    return "c_" + uuid.uuid4().hex[:24]

610	def ingest_from_codex(
611	    upload_id: str,
612	    *,
613	    codex_db_url: Optional[str] = None,
614	    use_llm: bool = False,
615	    max_claims: int = 40,
616	    dry_run: bool = False,
617	    organization_slug_filter: Optional[str] = None,
618	) -> IngestFromCodexResult:
619	    """
620	    Read a Codex Upload, extract claims, write Conclusions back into the
621	    same DB. Returns a small result object so the CLI can print a summary.
622	    """
623	    url = _resolve_codex_db_url(codex_db_url)
624	    conn = _open_codex_connection(url)
625	    try:
626	        cur = conn.cursor(cursor_factory=psycopg2.extras.RealDictCursor)
627	
628	        # ── Fetch the upload ─────────────────────────────────────────────
629	        cur.execute(
630	            '''SELECT id, "organizationId", "founderId", title, "textContent",
631	                      status, "mimeType", "originalName", "filePath", "fileSize"
632	               FROM "Upload" WHERE id = %s''',
633	            (upload_id,),
634	        )
635	        row = cur.fetchone()
636	        if row is None:
637	            raise RuntimeError(f"Upload {upload_id} not found in the Codex DB.")
638	
639	        # ── Fetch-and-extract via the MIME dispatcher ───────────────────
640	        # ``fetch_upload_content`` returns TextContent when the DB has
641	        # textContent populated, or BinaryContent (bytes + mime) when the
642	        # row points at Supabase Storage / a local path. ``dispatch``
643	        # then routes to the appropriate extractor (passthrough for text,
644	        # Whisper for audio — see prompt 02, pypdf for PDF — see prompt
645	        # 03). Failures are marked on the Upload row with a structured
646	        # reason so the dashboard (prompt 04) can surface them.
647	        from noosphere.extractors import (
648	            ExtractionFailed,
649	            UnsupportedMimeType,
650	            dispatch,
651	        )
652	        from noosphere.extractors._content_fetcher import fetch_upload_content
653	
654	        # Mark `extracting` as we enter the MIME dispatch. This is the
655	        # stage where Whisper/pypdf run; the Codex dashboard shows an
656	        # amber pulsing badge so the founder can see the pipeline is
657	        # alive before any LLM calls happen.
658	        if not dry_run:
659	            cur.execute(
660	                'UPDATE "Upload" SET status = %s WHERE id = %s',
661	                ("extracting", upload_id),
662	            )
663	            conn.commit()
664	
665	        try:
666	            content = fetch_upload_content(row, conn)
667	            extracted = dispatch(content)
668	        except (ExtractionFailed, UnsupportedMimeType) as exc:
669	            reason = (
670	                f"unsupported_mime: {exc}"
671	                if isinstance(exc, UnsupportedMimeType)
672	                else f"extraction_failed: {exc}"
673	            )
674	            if not dry_run:
675	                cur.execute(
676	                    '''UPDATE "Upload"
677	                       SET status = %s, "errorMessage" = %s
678	                       WHERE id = %s''',
679	                    ("failed", _db_safe(reason, cap=400), upload_id),
680	                )
681	                conn.commit()
682	            raise
683	
684	        text = extracted.text
685	        print(
686	            f"extraction method={extracted.extraction_method} "
687	            f"source_format={extracted.source_format} "
688	            f"warnings={len(extracted.warnings)}"
689	        )
690	        for w in extracted.warnings:
691	            print(f"  warning: {w}")
692	
693	        if not text or not text.strip():
694	            raise RuntimeError(
695	                f"Upload {upload_id}: extractor {extracted.extraction_method!r} "
696	                "returned empty text."
697	            )
698	
699	        # Optional sanity check on tenant if the caller passed a slug — protects
700	        # against running this against the wrong Codex tenant by mistake.
701	        if organization_slug_filter:
702	            cur.execute(
703	                'SELECT slug FROM "Organization" WHERE id = %s',
704	                (row["organizationId"],),
705	            )
706	            org = cur.fetchone()
707	            if not org or org["slug"] != organization_slug_filter:
708	                raise RuntimeError(
709	                    f"Upload {upload_id} belongs to org {org['slug'] if org else '?'}, "
710	                    f"not the expected slug '{organization_slug_filter}'."
711	                )
712	
713	        # ── Intermediate beat: text is ready, claim extraction next ──────
714	        # The dashboard shows `awaiting_ingest` momentarily between the
715	        # two heavy stages. For uploads with no PromptSeparator/LLM work
716	        # this flashes by — the point is that if the pipeline stalls,
717	        # the stall point is visible.
718	        if not dry_run:
719	            cur.execute(
720	                'UPDATE "Upload" SET status = %s WHERE id = %s',
721	                ("awaiting_ingest", upload_id),
722	            )
723	            conn.commit()
724	
725	        # ── Mark processing (non-dry only) ───────────────────────────────
726	        if not dry_run:
727	            cur.execute(
728	                'UPDATE "Upload" SET status = %s WHERE id = %s',
729	                ("processing", upload_id),
730	            )
731	            conn.commit()
732	
733	        # ── Strip interview prompts / external quotes BEFORE extraction ─
734	        # Defense-in-depth against misattributing external text as
735	        # founder claims. Failures here log & fall through to raw text.
736	        try:
737	            from noosphere.mitigations.prompt_separator import PromptSeparator
738	            separated = PromptSeparator().separate(text, source_type="written")
739	            print(
740	                f"prompt_separator founder_sections={len(separated.founder_sections)} "
741	                f"prompt_sections={len(separated.prompt_sections)} "
742	                f"confidence={separated.confidence:.2f}"
743	            )
744	            if separated.founder_sections:
745	                text = separated.founder_text
746	        except Exception as sep_err:
747	            print(f"prompt_separator_failed: {sep_err}")
748	
749	        # ── Extract claims ───────────────────────────────────────────────
750	        if use_llm:
751	            claims = llm_extract_claims(text, upload_id=upload_id, max_chunks=10)
752	            mode = "llm"
753	        else:
754	            claims = naive_extract_claims(text, max_claims=max_claims)
755	            mode = "naive"
756	
757	        if dry_run:
758	            return IngestFromCodexResult(
759	                upload_id=upload_id,
760	                title=row["title"] or row["originalName"] or upload_id,
761	                num_claims_extracted=len(claims),
762	                num_conclusions_written=0,
763	                num_contradictions_written=0,
764	                num_open_questions_written=0,
765	                num_research_suggestions_written=0,
766	                dry_run=True,
767	                mode=mode,
768	            )
769	
770	        # ── Insert conclusions (with dedup + source linking) ─────────────
771	        # We also track a parallel list of (claim_index → conclusion_id)
772	        # so we can cite the right Conclusion rows from contradictions
773	        # and open questions below. Claims that are too short to insert
774	        # won't appear in this map (their index maps to None).
775	        #
776	        # Dedup pass: before inserting a Conclusion we look for an
777	        # existing row in this org with the same normalised text. If one
778	        # exists, we DON'T duplicate — we just add this upload as an
779	        # additional source via ConclusionSource. That's the mechanic
780	        # that lets a Conclusion survive when one of its two sources is
781	        # later deleted. A fresh insert writes both the Conclusion AND a
782	        # ConclusionSource row.
783	        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
784	        written = 0  # NEW conclusions inserted
785	        deduped = 0  # claims that matched an existing conclusion
786	        claim_idx_to_conclusion_id: list[str | None] = [None] * len(claims)
787	        # Pre-compute a sanitized source label we'll reuse in every row's
788	        # rationale/sessionLabel. Cap at 300 so a giant filename can't blow
789	        # the text column.
790	        source_label = _db_safe(
791	            row["title"] or row["originalName"] or upload_id,
792	            cap=300,
793	        )
794	        for idx, claim in enumerate(claims):
795	            claim_text = _db_safe(claim.text, cap=4_000).strip()
796	            if len(claim_text) < 20:
797	                continue
798	            normalized = _normalize_claim_text(claim_text)
799	            if not normalized:
800	                continue
801	
802	            # Dedup lookup — matching is scoped to the same org.
803	            cur.execute(
804	                '''SELECT id FROM "Conclusion"
805	                   WHERE "organizationId" = %s AND "normalizedText" = %s
806	                   LIMIT 1''',
807	                (row["organizationId"], normalized),
808	            )
809	            existing = cur.fetchone()
810	
811	            if existing:
812	                cid = existing["id"]
813	                # Attach this upload as another source. ON CONFLICT is
814	                # a defensive no-op in case the user is re-running the
815	                # ingest for the same upload.
816	                cur.execute(
817	                    '''INSERT INTO "ConclusionSource"
818	                       ("conclusionId", "uploadId", "createdAt")
819	                       VALUES (%s, %s, %s)
820	                       ON CONFLICT ("conclusionId", "uploadId") DO NOTHING''',
821	                    (cid, upload_id, now),
822	                )
823	                claim_idx_to_conclusion_id[idx] = cid
824	                deduped += 1
825	                continue
826	
827	            cid = _short_cuid_like()
828	            noosphere_id = f"ing_{upload_id}_{idx}"
829	            cur.execute(
830	                '''INSERT INTO "Conclusion"
831	                   (id, "organizationId", "noosphereId", text, "normalizedText",
832	                    "confidenceTier", rationale, "supportingPrincipleIds",
833	                    "evidenceChainClaimIds", "dissentClaimIds",
834	                    confidence, "topicHint", "createdAt")
835	                   VALUES (%s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s)
836	                   ON CONFLICT ("noosphereId") DO NOTHING''',
837	                (
838	                    cid,
839	                    row["organizationId"],
840	                    noosphere_id,
841	                    claim_text,
842	                    normalized,
843	                    "open",
844	                    _db_safe(f"Extracted from upload: {source_label}", cap=500),
845	                    json.dumps([]),
846	                    json.dumps([]),
847	                    json.dumps([]),
848	                    0.5,
849	                    _db_safe(claim.claim_type, cap=64),
850	                    now,
851	                ),
852	            )
853	            if cur.rowcount == 1:
854	                written += 1
855	                claim_idx_to_conclusion_id[idx] = cid
856	                cur.execute(
857	                    '''INSERT INTO "ConclusionSource"
858	                       ("conclusionId", "uploadId", "createdAt")
859	                       VALUES (%s, %s, %s)
860	                       ON CONFLICT ("conclusionId", "uploadId") DO NOTHING''',
861	                    (cid, upload_id, now),
862	                )
863	
864	        # ── Contradiction detection ─────────────────────────────────────
865	        # Always run the heuristic pass (cheap, no deps). If LLM is
866	        # available AND use_llm=True, do the combined contradictions +
867	        # questions + research pass for higher quality.
868	        contradictions_written = 0
869	        open_questions_written = 0
870	        research_suggestions_written = 0
871	
872	        heuristic_pairs = naive_detect_contradictions(claims)
873	
874	        llm_payload: dict | None = None
875	        llm_error: str | None = None
876	        if use_llm:
877	            try:
878	                # Pull up to 40 recent firm conclusions for cross-upload checking.
879	                cur.execute(
880	                    '''SELECT id, text FROM "Conclusion"
881	                       WHERE "organizationId" = %s
882	                         AND "noosphereId" NOT LIKE %s
883	                       ORDER BY "createdAt" DESC LIMIT 40''',
884	                    (row["organizationId"], f"ing_{upload_id}_%"),
885	                )
886	                firm_conclusions = [(r["id"], r["text"]) for r in cur.fetchall()]
887	                llm_payload = llm_detect_contradictions_and_questions(
888	                    claims,
889	                    firm_conclusions,
890	                    upload_title=row["title"] or row["originalName"] or upload_id,
891	                )
892	            except Exception as exc:
893	                # LLM failure is non-fatal — we still have the heuristic
894	                # contradictions and the inserted Conclusions. Record the
895	                # reason so the caller can surface it in the process log.
896	                llm_error = f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"
897	
898	        # ── Write contradictions (both sources merged) ──────────────────
899	        # Every contradiction carries `sourceUploadId = upload_id` — it's
900	        # the product of THIS ingestion run. Cross-upload contradictions
901	        # (where claim B is an existing firm Conclusion from a DIFFERENT
902	        # upload) also get the current upload as their source, since that
903	        # upload is the one that surfaced the conflict. Deleting either
904	        # upload will remove the contradiction — the current upload via
905	        # its `sourceUploadId`, the other upload via claimBId being
906	        # orphan-deleted when ITS source Conclusion loses all sources.
907	        for pair in heuristic_pairs:
908	            a_cid = claim_idx_to_conclusion_id[pair.a_idx]
909	            b_cid = (
910	                claim_idx_to_conclusion_id[pair.b_idx]
911	                if pair.b_idx is not None
912	                else pair.b_existing_id
913	            )
914	            if not a_cid or not b_cid:
915	                continue
916	            cur.execute(
917	                '''INSERT INTO "Contradiction"
918	                   (id, "organizationId", "claimAId", "claimBId", severity,
919	                    "sixLayerJson", narrative, "sourceUploadId", "createdAt")
920	                   VALUES (%s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s)''',
921	                (
922	                    "x_" + uuid.uuid4().hex[:24],
923	                    row["organizationId"],
924	                    a_cid,
925	                    b_cid,
926	                    float(pair.severity),
927	                    json.dumps({"source": "heuristic"}),
928	                    _db_safe(pair.narrative, cap=2_000),
929	                    upload_id,
930	                    now,
931	                ),
932	            )
933	            contradictions_written += cur.rowcount
934	
935	        # LLM contradictions (within-upload).
936	        if llm_payload:
937	            for item in llm_payload.get("contradictions", []):
938	                try:
939	                    a = int(item["a"])
940	                    b = int(item["b"])
941	                    sev = float(item.get("severity", 0.6))
942	                    narrative = str(item.get("narrative", ""))[:500]
943	                except (KeyError, ValueError, TypeError):
944	                    continue
